title: lithocarpus jacksonianus
intro: Lithocarpus jacksonianus, also known as the Jackson's oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to China. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The bark is rich in tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting immune function. Lithocarpus jacksonianus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used in the form of dried bark, capsules, or extracts for medicinal purposes.
